task_completion.visual_evidence: The Microsoft Edge browser window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The agent configures User-Agent spoofing to emulate Internet Explorer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Edge Developer Tools are open, showing the 'Network conditions' tab (or similar settings) where the User-Agent is set to a custom string containing 'MSIE' or 'Trident'.

task_completion.description: The agent successfully navigates to the legacy portal without being blocked.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Edge address bar shows 'localhost:8000' or '127.0.0.1:8000' and the main portal page is loaded, bypassing any browser compatibility error messages.

task_completion.description: The Manifest Code is successfully retrieved and displayed on the portal page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The loaded portal page clearly displays an alphanumeric Manifest Code (format: MFST-<4-digits>-<4-digits>).

task_completion.description: The Manifest Code is saved to the required text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window shows the file '/home/ga/Desktop/manifest_code.txt' containing the exact Manifest Code seen on the portal page.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the portal using the Microsoft Edge GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Microsoft Edge graphical interface being used to navigate and configure settings, rather than terminal commands like 'curl' being used to spoof the user agent and fetch the page.

integrity.description: The agent navigated to the actual local server.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's address bar shows 'localhost:8000' or '127.0.0.1:8000', not a local file path (e.g., 'file://').

integrity.description: The saved manifest code matches the one generated by the portal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code saved in '/home/ga/Desktop/manifest_code.txt' is identical to the one displayed on the portal page in the browser, proving it was genuinely retrieved and not fabricated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The portal is hosted at http://localhost:8000. It requires a User-Agent containing 'MSIE' or 'Trident' to bypass the compatibility check. Upon successful access, it displays a Manifest Code in the format MFST-<4-digit-random>-<4-digit-timestamp>. The agent must save this exact code to /home/ga/Desktop/manifest_code.txt.
